# xcforge full LLM context

> xcforge is a native Swift CLI and MCP server for local iOS workflows. It helps AI-assisted developers move from generated code to verified outcomes across build, test, simulator, UI automation, logs, screenshots, and diagnosis loops.

## How to use this document

- Use this file for broad product and docs understanding.
- Use /docs pages for focused implementation details.
- Treat repository files as canonical when docs and code differ.

## Product summary

- Primary surface: command-line tool for local iOS execution workflows.
- Parallel surface: MCP server exposing a corresponding capability set.
- Core value: verifiable local outcomes with inspectable evidence and conservative trust signals.

## Quick start

Install with Homebrew:

- brew tap justinthevoid/tap && brew install xcforge

First verification loop:

- xcforge --help
- xcforge build-test --json
- xcforge test failures --include-console --json

Optional simulator and evidence checks:

- xcforge sim list --json
- xcforge log start --mode smart --json
- xcforge screenshot capture --json
- xcforge log read --include app --include crashes --json
- xcforge log stop --json

## Documentation map

- [Docs overview](/docs/): Entry point for CLI and MCP documentation.
- [Getting started](/docs/getting-started/): Install, configure, and verify baseline workflows.
- [CLI command groups](/docs/guides/cli-command-groups/): Map command groups to build/test/simulator/diagnosis goals.
- [Workflow patterns](/docs/guides/workflow-patterns/): Practical execution loops and handoff-safe patterns.
- [MCP server overview](/docs/reference/mcp-server-overview/): Server identity, transport model, and capability framing.
- [MCP client configuration](/docs/reference/mcp-client-configuration/): Supported host configuration examples and setup guidance.
- [CLI and MCP parity](/docs/reference/cli-mcp-parity/): Interface alignment expectations and caveats.
- [Proof provenance and freshness](/docs/proof-provenance/): Trust marker interpretation and source reconciliation guidance.

## Common evaluation questions

What problem does xcforge solve?
- It provides one local execution contract for iOS agent workflows so generated changes can be validated through repeatable evidence-producing commands.

Who is it for?
- Technical evaluators and AI-assisted iOS developers who need credible, local, inspectable execution rather than ungrounded claims.

How should agents decide between CLI and MCP?
- Prefer whichever interface is already integrated in the environment. For behavior differences or edge cases, consult CLI and MCP parity docs.

How should trust-sensitive claims be handled?
- Follow proof provenance guidance and avoid asserting capabilities without observable execution evidence.
